validation_evidence.result: RED accepted: 55 passed, 4 failed, 59 total tests across 3 files.
validation_evidence.setup_health: No syntax, import, dependency, or test setup failures observed.
failing_behavior_categories: Recommended-skill priority is not surfaced as attention-only runtime guidance.
Workflow prompt/policy guidance does not explain priority as non-safety-overriding metadata.
Runtime prompt does not instruct material skill audit outcomes: used, relevant-skipped, and relevant-unavailable.
Runtime prompt does not state explicit SkillTool auto-run and nested/depth guardrails.
expected_failure_reasons: workflowRuntimeService formatRecommendedSkillsPromptBlock currently lists status/source diagnostics but omits priority semantics.
workflowRuntimeService assemblePrompt currently returns scheduledToolCalls: [] but does not include explicit auto-run, nested invocation, or depth-limit language.
workflowRuntimeService prompt guidance does not name the bounded soft-audit outcome vocabulary.
workflowToolPolicy getWorkflowPromptToolGuidance states recommended skills do not grant permissions, but does not describe priority as attention-only metadata.
passing_coverage: Resolver now covers stable identity/provenance for project-local, bundled, plugin, managed, and MCP recommended skill references.
Existing permission and completion tests remained intact outside the intended RED assertions.

MP-005.evidence: Tests keep recommended skills soft by asserting priority is attention metadata and does not grant SkillTool access or permissions.

MP-006.evidence: Runtime RED test requires bounded material audit vocabulary for used, relevant-skipped, and relevant-unavailable rather than a mechanical unused checklist.

CA-004.evidence: Tool policy test asserts priority recommendations do not add SkillTool to workflow-scoped tools; existing permission tests still pass.

CA-010.evidence: Runtime RED test requires explicit auto-run, nested invocation, and depth guardrail language while scheduledToolCalls remains empty.

CA-001.evidence: workflowPhaseSkillResolver.test.ts adds a stable identity/provenance matrix for project-local, bundled, plugin, managed, and MCP skill references.

CA-002.evidence: workflowToolPolicy.test.ts asserts priority must be described as attention metadata, not a safety override or permission grant.

CA-003.evidence: Existing resolver diagnostics for missing, ambiguous, unsupported-source, plugin-disabled, and invalid-reference remain in the targeted run and passed.

CA-004.evidence: Existing and new tool-policy assertions preserve SkillTool permission boundaries for recommended and priority recommendations.

CA-005.evidence: Existing runtime test for startPhase snapshotting recommended skill resolutions remained in the targeted run and passed.

CA-006.evidence: New runtime RED test requires prompt guidance for material used, relevant-skipped, and relevant-unavailable audit outcomes.

CA-010.evidence: New runtime RED test requires explicit recommended-skill auto-run and nested/depth guardrail language with no scheduled tool calls.

recovery_guidance: T008 should update workflowRuntimeService recommended-skill prompt assembly to include priority labels as attention-only metadata and not as safety or permission overrides.
T008 should add bounded soft-audit guidance for material used, relevant-skipped, and relevant-unavailable skill outcomes without mechanically listing every unused recommendation.
T008 should make auto-run and recursion/depth guardrails explicit while preserving scheduledToolCalls as an empty array for recommended skills.
T008 should update workflowToolPolicy prompt guidance so priority recommendations remain advisory and do not expose SkillTool globally.
